name: MetaMask SDK
what_it_does: Provides the MetaMask SDK and EIP-1193 Ethereum provider API for integrating the world's most widely-used crypto wallet into dApps across browser extensions, mobile apps, and desktop environments.
use_cases: Connect a web dApp to the user's MetaMask browser extension to read wallet address and chain without any backend
Request transaction signatures from MetaMask users for ETH transfers and ERC-20 token approvals
Prompt users to sign arbitrary messages (EIP-712 typed data) for gasless transactions or off-chain authentication
Use MetaMask SDK to establish connections from mobile apps and non-browser environments to the MetaMask mobile wallet
Switch or add custom EVM-compatible networks (Polygon, Arbitrum, BSC) to the user's MetaMask from your dApp
not_for: Backend or server-side agents that need to sign transactions programmatically — MetaMask requires a human user to approve all signing operations
dApps needing to support non-MetaMask wallets broadly — use WalletConnect or Dynamic for multi-wallet support
best_when: Best when targeting the largest installed base of crypto users (MetaMask has 30M+ users) and need tight, well-tested integration with the EIP-1193 standard in a browser environment.
avoid_when: Avoid when building backend automation that needs programmatic transaction signing, or when broad multi-wallet support is more important than deep MetaMask-specific integration.

auth.notes: No API key required. Authentication is wallet-native: the user's Ethereum address serves as identity, and signatures prove ownership. No backend auth to manage.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free to use; no usage caps on the SDK or provider API
pricing.paid_tiers: None — MetaMask is free for dApp developers. Revenue comes from MetaMask's built-in token swap fee (0.875%).
pricing.notes: No cost to integrate. MetaMask's business model is swap fees from end users, not developer fees.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits on the provider API itself; underlying RPC node limits may apply depending on configured network endpoint

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Provider errors follow EIP-1193 error codes (4001 user rejected, 4100 unauthorized, etc.) which are standardized and predictable. MetaMask-specific errors can be less clearly documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations (eth_accounts, eth_chainId) are fully idempotent. State-changing calls (eth_sendTransaction) are not — duplicate calls will attempt duplicate transactions.
known_agent_gotchas: MetaMask is fundamentally browser/UI-bound — server-side or headless agent usage is not supported; use ethers.js or viem with a private key for programmatic signing instead
User can reject any request (error code 4001); agents must handle rejection gracefully and not retry without user re-initiation
MetaMask SDK requires the user to have MetaMask installed or falls back to deep link on mobile — no fallback wallet is provided unlike WalletConnect

security_score_components.security_notes: Cryptographically strong security model — private keys never leave the MetaMask extension/mobile app. All transactions require explicit user approval. Open-source with extensive security audits.
